name: Cucumber Yogurt Soup with Fresh Mint
recipeIngredient: 1 Large Cucumber (or 2 medium cucumbers), peeled, seeded and coarsely chopped
Salt and freshly ground white pepper to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor or blender, process the cucumber, mint, scallions, dill, and garlic until well combined but not smooth.
2. Add the yogurt and continue pureeing until completely smooth.
3. Chill for 4–6 hours (or overnight).
4. Adjust seasonings as needed and serve the soup with garnishes of sliced cucumbers, radishes, chopped scallions, and grated hard-boiled egg (optional).
Serves: 4
This light soup uses low-fat yogurt instead of sour cream, reducing salt since the fresh herbs naturally enhance flavor. Fresh mint is more potent than dried; frozen portions can be made by wrapping bunches in foil before storing. Cucumbers should be kept separate from ethylene-producing fruits and vegetables to maintain crispness. Smaller pickling cucumbers work well as a substitute.
